questions.question: At what percentage of a plan limit should you initiate an expansion conversation with a customer?

options.text: 100% — wait until they hit the wall

questions.explanation: The 80% rule is optimal because the customer has breathing room and the conversation feels helpful rather than desperate. At 100%, they're frustrated and you're reacting to a problem. Conversion rates are 40-60% at 80% vs. 15-25% after hitting the wall.

questions.question: What makes a usage trigger 'usage-based' rather than 'outcome-based'?

options.text: It tracks how the customer uses the product, not what results they achieve
It requires manual detection by the founder
It only applies to enterprise customers

questions.explanation: Usage-based triggers track measurable product behaviors — seats used, storage consumed, API calls made — while outcome-based triggers track business results like revenue growth or churn reduction. Usage triggers are objective and product-driven.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic of an effective usage trigger?

options.text: It fires after the customer has already churned
It is objective, measurable, predictive, and timely
It only works with annual billing cycles

questions.explanation: Effective usage triggers are objective (no interpretation required), measurable (trackable in your systems), predictive (correlate with successful upgrades historically), and timely (fire before frustration, not after the customer hits a wall).

questions.question: Waiting until a customer hits 100% of their plan limit before reaching out results in higher conversion rates than reaching out at 80%.
questions.explanation: False. Reaching out at 80% converts at 40-60%, while reaching out after they hit the wall converts at only 15-25%. At 100%, customers are frustrated and may already be evaluating competitors.

questions.question: Identify 3 usage-based triggers specific to your product. For each trigger, define the threshold that should fire the alert, how you would detect it technically, and what expansion conversation you would initiate.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response identifies product-specific triggers (not generic ones), defines concrete thresholds (e.g., '80% of 50 project limit'), describes a realistic detection method (database query, analytics tool, or billing system), and outlines a helpful expansion conversation rather than a hard sell.
